entities.name: RAINWATER HARVESTING
entities.description: Decentralized approach to collect and store rainwater to mitigate drought and manage water resources.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall leading to water shortages in drylands.

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Arid or semi-arid regions characterized by a lack of sufficient water for agriculture.

entities.name: HYDROLOGICAL PROCESSES
entities.description: The movement, distribution, and management of water, including infiltration and runoff.

entities.name: META-ANALYSIS
entities.description: A quantitative statistical analysis that combines results from multiple scientific studies.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL WATERSHEDS
entities.description: Land areas that channel rainfall and snowmelt to common water bodies, used for farming.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Precipitation falling as rain, serving as a primary driver for runoff and soil loss.

entities.name: SOIL TYPES
entities.description: Different classifications of soil based on their physical and chemical properties.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Tropical regions characterized by low to moderate annual rainfall.

entities.name: RAINFALL-RUNOFF-SOIL LOSS RELATIONSHIP
entities.description: The correlation between precipitation levels, the resulting surface runoff, and the amount of soil eroded.

entities.name: SURFACE RUNOFF
entities.description: Water from rain or snowmelt that flows over the land surface instead of infiltrating into the soil.

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The removal of the top layer of soil from the land surface, often caused by water erosion.

entities.name: INDIAN SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The semi-arid tropical regions specifically located within India.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The volume and accessibility of water stored in underground aquifers.

entities.name: CROP INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of increasing crop productivity or the number of crops grown per unit area.

entities.name: FARMERS' INCOMES
entities.description: The financial earnings generated by agricultural producers from their crops.
